title: Producer Company Registration Online in India | Cost & Consultant
mainHeading: Producer Company Registration in India (Online Service)

hero.headline: Empower Farmers with Legal Identity through a Producer Company
hero.subtext: Professionals Law helps agricultural producers legally register and manage their Producer Company with full compliance, professional guidance and documentation support.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A Producer Company is a special category of company registered under Section 581B of the Companies Act, 1956 to allow farmers, agriculturists and producers to leverage a corporate business group. It permits them to organize and commercialize their agricultural and allied operations under a corporate identity and offers them a separate legal identity. With Professionals Law, you can establish a Producer Company and in accordance with the legal norms established by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, effectively and securely register the Producer Company with complete legal assistance and documentation backing.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: A Producer Company is essential to provide a collective identity and professionally organized business to small and marginal farmers or producers. They readily have access to professional management, corporate infrastructure and institutional credit. It enables them to defeat intermediaries, have more power while bargaining, earn from the inputs and acquire the recovery of government strategies and financial support. Professionals Law offers support for producer communities through the legal finality of their operational operations, assisting in availing institutional credit, professional management and long-term concern.

body.title: Collective Empowerment
body.description: It enables farmers and producers to pool resources, minimize middlemen and operate on cooperative principles for better returns and shared benefits.

body.title: Legal Recognition
body.description: Provides a legal identity to the collective, enhancing credibility, transparency and access to institutional finance and government grants.

body.title: Limited Liability Protection
body.description: Members’ personal assets are safeguarded and liability is limited to the extent of their shareholding in the company.

body.title: Tax Benefits
body.description: Certain agricultural income of Producer Companies is exempt from tax, making it a cost-effective model for producer organizations.

body.title: Market Access
body.description: Facilitates better market linkages and logistics support, enabling members to directly reach wholesale buyers, retailers and exporters.

body.title: Professional Management
body.description: Ensures the inclusion of professional management practices with provisions for appointing CEOs and trained staff for smooth operations.

criteria: A minimum of 10 individual producers or 2 producer institutions or a combination of both is required.
All members must be primary producers, such as farmers, artisans or agriculturists.
The primary objective must be production, harvesting, processing, procurement, grading, pooling, handling, marketing, selling or export of primary produce.

documents: PAN and Aadhaar of all the members and directors
Email id and mobile number of all the members and directors
Passport-size photographs of all the members and directors
Proof of registered office (rent agreement or ownership proof)
Professionals Law ensures that all documents are prepared, verified and filed correctly for timely registration.

body.description: Professionals Law assesses member eligibility and provides strategic consultation.
DSC and DIN are obtained for proposed directors.
Application is filed with MCA for name reservation using RUN (Reserve Unique Name) service.
Preparation of MOA, AOA and Producer Company charter.
Filing of SPICe+ forms and incorporation documents online.
MCA issues a Certificate of Incorporation, making the Producer Company operational.
Professionals Law assists with PAN, TAN, bank account and statutory compliance setup.

penalties.heading: Penalties
body: Monetary fines on the company and its officers
Disqualification of directors for continuous default
Late filing fees and penalties for delay in statutory filings
Suspension of business activities or cancellation of registration in extreme cases
With Professionals Law, you can stay fully compliant and avoid these risks.

body.question: Who can form a Producer Company?
body.answer: A minimum of 10 producers or 2 producer institutions can form a Producer Company.

body.question: What is considered a primary producer?
body.answer: A primary producer includes farmers, dairy owners, fishermen, weavers and artisans who are directly involved in production activities.

body.question: Is it mandatory to have an office address for registration?
body.answer: Yes, a registered office address is mandatory for incorporating a Producer Company.

body.question: Can foreign nationals be members of a Producer Company?
body.answer: No, only Indian citizens involved in primary production can be members.

body.question: What are the post-registration compliances?
body.answer: Maintaining books of accounts, annual filings, board meetings and audits are mandatory.

body.question: Is there any tax benefit for Producer Companies?
body.answer: Yes, agricultural income earned by Producer Companies is generally exempt from income tax.

body.question: Can a Producer Company accept deposits from its members?
body.answer: Yes, with certain conditions and approvals under the Companies Act.

body.question: What is the minimum capital required?
body.answer: There is no specific minimum capital, but the company must have a feasible capital structure as per MCA guidelines.

body.question: Can a Producer Company be converted into a regular company?
body.answer: No, a Producer Company cannot be converted into a regular company.
